trips.name: Bavarian Classics
trips.description: The quintessential Bavarian road trip sweeps through fairytale castles, Alpine meadows, and pristine lakes. From Munich's cosmopolitan energy to the soaring peaks above Garmisch-Partenkirchen, every mile reveals a postcard landscape.
trips.cover_search_query: Neuschwanstein Castle autumn Bavaria Germany

trips.name: Berchtesgaden Alpine Escape
trips.description: This southern Bavarian loop dips into the dramatic Berchtesgaden Alps, skirts the emerald Königssee, and passes through the salt-mining town of Bad Reichenhall. The scenery is some of the most spectacular in Germany.
highlights: Königssee boat trip to St. Bartholomä
trips.cover_search_query: Königssee lake St Bartholomä Bavaria Germany

trips.name: Bavarian Lake Country
trips.description: A gentle tour of Bavaria's glittering pre-Alpine lakes — Starnberger See, Ammersee, Tegernsee, and Schliersee — each ringed by charming villages, beer gardens, and mountain backdrops. Perfect for swimming, cycling, and Bavarian hospitality.
trips.cover_search_query: Tegernsee village Bavaria Alps reflection Germany

trips.name: Zugspitze & Wetterstein
trips.description: Germany's highest peak anchors this compact Alpine circuit, taking in the Eibsee's turquoise waters, the gorges of the Partnachklamm, and the meadows of the Loisachtal. An intense mountain experience in a short driving distance.
trips.cover_search_query: Zugspitze summit Germany highest peak Bavaria

trips.name: Allgäu & Oberschwaben
trips.description: Rolling green hills, cow-bells, cheese dairies, and the dramatic Allgäu Alps define this southwest Bavarian tour. The route also dips into Upper Swabia's remarkable concentration of Baroque monasteries and pilgrimage churches.
trips.cover_search_query: Oberstdorf village Allgäu Alps Germany

trips.name: Lower Bavaria Danube Loop
trips.description: The Danube carves through the gentle hills of Lower Bavaria past Romanesque abbeys, medieval market towns, and the confluence of the rivers Inn and Danube at Passau. This is rural Bavaria at its most unhurried and authentic.
trips.cover_search_query: Passau three rivers confluence Danube Bavaria Germany

trips.name: Munich to Salzburg Scenic Route
trips.description: Rather than taking the motorway, this scenic route between two great cities hugs the Alpine foothills through market towns, onion-domed churches, and lakeside resorts. The Chiemgau countryside is Bavaria's breadbasket at its most serene.
trips.cover_search_query: Chiemsee island Bavaria Germany aerial view

trips.name: Bavarian Forest National Park
trips.description: Germany's oldest national park stretches along the Czech border in a vast arc of ancient forest, granite peaks, and glacial lakes. The route passes through glass-blowing villages, the Bohemian market town of Deggendorf, and the medieval gem of Cham.
trips.cover_search_query: Bavarian Forest National Park autumn misty Germany

trips.name: Munich & Oktoberfest Country
trips.description: A short but deeply Bavarian loop from Munich through the heartland of beer culture — Freising with its ancient cathedral brewery, Erding, and the hop-growing fields of the Hallertau. Ideal for visitors who want the culture beyond the crowds.
trips.cover_search_query: Landshut castle Bavaria Germany medieval town

trips.name: German Alpine Road
trips.description: The Deutsche Alpenstraße runs the entire length of the Bavarian Alps from Lindau on Lake Constance to Berchtesgaden. This classic touring road links every major Alpine resort, castle, and high pass in an unforgettable east–west traverse.
trips.cover_search_query: German Alpine Road Deutsche Alpenstrasse Bavaria mountain road

trips.name: Ingolstadt & Upper Danube Valley
trips.description: Upstream from Regensburg, the Danube narrows into dramatic limestone gorges flanked by cliffs and castles. This circuit from Ingolstadt visits Kelheim's Liberation Hall, the Altmühltal Nature Park, and the monastic heartland of Bavaria.
trips.cover_search_query: Altmühltal valley limestone cliffs Bavaria Germany

trips.name: Augsburg & Swabian Bavaria
trips.description: Augsburg, Germany's third-oldest city, anchors a route through Swabian Bavaria that spans Renaissance merchant palaces, the world's oldest social housing, and the Lech River wetlands — a quieter side of Bavaria often overlooked by tourists.
trips.cover_search_query: Augsburg Rathaus Renaissance Bavaria Germany

trips.name: Munich Day-Trips Circuit
trips.description: Munich sits at the centre of an extraordinary ring of day-trip destinations — this route strings them together into a satisfying loop, from the concentration camp memorial at Dachau to the Roman ruins at Augsburg and the fairy-tale castles of the Royal Road.
trips.cover_search_query: Wieskirche interior Baroque Bavaria Germany UNESCO

trips.name: Classic Romantic Road
trips.description: Germany's most famous themed tourist route connects the Main Valley at Würzburg with the Alps at Füssen through a procession of medieval walled towns, Baroque palaces, and vine-covered hillsides. The Romantische Straße is a rite of passage.
highlights: Rothenburg ob der Tauber medieval walls
trips.cover_search_query: Rothenburg ob der Tauber medieval town walls Germany

trips.name: Romantic Road North
trips.description: The northern half of the Romantic Road follows the Tauber Valley through rolling Franconian hills and a string of half-timbered market towns. Würzburg's Baroque splendour gives way to the perfectly preserved medieval streetscapes of Rothenburg.
trips.cover_search_query: Würzburg Residenz vineyard Main river Germany

trips.name: Romantic Road South — Castle Country
trips.description: The southern stretch of the Romantic Road races through Swabian towns to a grand Alpine finale at Füssen. The climax is the twin castles of Neuschwanstein and Hohenschwangau, rising above an Alpsee lake that mirrors their silhouettes.
highlights: Füssen old town and Hohes Schloss
trips.cover_search_query: Neuschwanstein Hohenschwangau castles Alpsee Bavaria Germany

trips.name: Tauber Valley Wine Route
trips.description: The Tauber River meanders through a valley of vineyards, half-timbered villages, and Romanesque churches that somehow escaped the destruction of the Second World War. This gentle loop is ideal for cyclists and wine lovers alike.
trips.cover_search_query: Tauber Valley vineyards Rothenburg Germany autumn

trips.name: Nördlingen & the Ries Crater
trips.description: Fifteen million years ago a meteorite created the circular Ries Crater; today its flat floor holds the perfectly round medieval town of Nördlingen. This short loop connects three Swabian gems set in an otherworldly geological landscape.
trips.cover_search_query: Nördlingen medieval walled town aerial Germany

trips.name: Rhine Gorge Classic
trips.description: The UNESCO Middle Rhine Valley squeezes between slate cliffs crowned with castle ruins for 65 kilometres from Bingen to Koblenz. This route combines river ferry crossings, wine-village strolls, and the legendary Loreley Rock.
trips.cover_search_query: Rhine Valley castles Middle Rhine gorge Germany

trips.name: Cologne to Koblenz Rhine Drive
trips.description: From Cologne's soaring Gothic cathedral the Rhine sweeps south through Bonn, Siebengebirge, and the volcanic Eifel foothills to the Moselle confluence at Koblenz. The drive mixes city culture with wild riverscape.
trips.cover_search_query: Cologne Cathedral Rhine river Germany golden hour

trips.name: Moselle Wine Road
trips.description: The Moselle River loops in extravagant curves through steep slate slopes planted with Riesling vines, the terraces seeming to defy gravity above the river. Half-timbered wine towns, ancient Roman monuments, and cobblestone weinstuben fill the route.
trips.cover_search_query: Moselle River vineyards Bernkastel wine village Germany

trips.name: Trier — Roman Heritage Circuit
trips.description: Trier was Rome's northern capital, and its concentration of UNESCO-listed Roman monuments is unmatched north of the Alps. This short circuit combines Trier's ancient splendour with Moselle wine villages and the rolling Hunsrück hills.
trips.cover_search_query: Trier Porta Nigra Roman gate Germany

trips.name: Nahe Valley & Rheingau Wine Route
trips.description: Germany's two most prestigious Riesling regions meet on this compact circuit from Mainz. The Rheingau's lordly monasteries and the Nahe's rocky gem mines set a dramatic stage for world-class white wine.
trips.cover_search_query: Rüdesheim Rheingau vineyards Rhine Germany

trips.name: Saar-Moselle Loop
trips.description: Where the Saar joins the Moselle near Konz, two great wine rivers converge in a landscape of extraordinary beauty. This loop explores both valleys, from Roman Trier to the spectacularly looping Saar at Serrig and Saarburg.
highlights: Saar River great loop at Serrig
trips.cover_search_query: Saar River loop bend Serrig vineyard Germany

trips.name: Rhine Castles & Wiesbaden
trips.description: Wiesbaden's elegant Wilhelminian spa architecture marks the start of a Rhine drive through the densest concentration of castle ruins in Europe. Each bend of the Middle Rhine reveals another ruined keep perched atop a vine-covered cliff.
trips.cover_search_query: Oberwesel Rhine castles town walls Germany

trips.name: Black Forest High Road
trips.description: The Schwarzwald-Hochstraße follows the ridge of the Northern Black Forest through viewpoints, moor ponds, and dense fir forests to the resort town of Freudenstadt. This is the classic Black Forest drive, full of panoramic pull-offs.
trips.cover_search_query: Black Forest Hochstraße misty road Germany

trips.name: Freiburg & Southern Black Forest
trips.description: Freiburg's sunny medieval city centre with its babbling Bächle streams anchors this tour of the Southern Black Forest. The route climbs to the Feldberg peak, lingers at the glacial Titisee, and descends through the gorges of the Wutachschlucht.
trips.cover_search_query: Titisee lake Black Forest village Germany

trips.name: Triberg & Cuckoo Clock Country
trips.description: The Central Black Forest is cuckoo clock territory, and Triberg's mighty waterfalls draw visitors year-round. This loop threads through open-air museum farmsteads, watchmaking towns, and the Kinzig Valley's half-timbered inns.
trips.cover_search_query: Triberg waterfalls Black Forest Germany

trips.name: Baden Wine Route
trips.description: The Badische Weinstraße runs along the warm western slopes of the Black Forest, one of Germany's sunniest wine regions. Pinot Noir, Pinot Gris, and Müller-Thurgau thrive here in a landscape of volcanic hills, spa towns, and Roman settlements.
trips.cover_search_query: Kaiserstuhl volcanic hill vineyards Baden Germany

trips.name: Black Forest Panoramic Road
trips.description: The Schwarzwald-Panoramastraße links the Northern and Central Black Forest with sweeping viewpoints over the Rhine plain and Vosges mountains. It passes through the spa grandeur of Baden-Baden and the lush Nagold Valley.
trips.cover_search_query: Baden-Baden Lichtentaler Allee autumn Germany

trips.name: Black Forest Valley Roads
trips.description: Where the rivers cut through the Black Forest they create narrow, moody valleys of great charm. This route descends three classic valleys — the Kinzig, Gutach, and Elz — each offering a different character of forest, waterfall, and village.
trips.cover_search_query: Kinzig Valley Black Forest autumn Germany

trips.name: Black Forest to Lake Constance
trips.description: A sweeping route from the dark forests and waterfalls of the Hochschwarzwald down to the sunshine and vineyards of Lake Constance (Bodensee). The contrast between dense Alpine forest and Mediterranean-feeling lakeside is extraordinary.
trips.cover_search_query: Meersburg Lake Constance Germany castle vineyard

trips.name: Dresden & Saxon Switzerland
trips.description: Dresden's Baroque skyline on the Elbe gives way to one of Europe's most dramatic national parks just 30km away. Sandstone pillars, table mountains, and the Bastei Bridge of Saxon Switzerland frame a journey between artistic grandeur and raw nature.
trips.cover_search_query: Bastei Bridge Saxon Switzerland Germany sandstone

trips.name: Elbe Valley Historic Route
trips.description: The Elbe from Dresden to Meissen and beyond traces one of Saxony's most historic corridors, passing vineyard terraces, Elbe castles, and the cathedral city of Meissen to the Weißes Elster confluence at Halle. History, wine, and riverside panoramas.
trips.cover_search_query: Meissen cathedral Albrechtsburg Elbe Germany

trips.name: Saxon Switzerland Rock Kingdom
trips.description: The Elbsandsteingebirge is Europe's rock climbing birthplace, where the sport was codified in 1910. This compact loop focuses on the national park's most dramatic formations — Bastei, Schrammsteine, Affensteine — woven between spa villages.
trips.cover_search_query: Saxon Switzerland Schrammsteine rock formations Elbe Germany

trips.name: Leipzig & Central Saxony
trips.description: Leipzig's dynamic cultural scene — Bach, Schiller, the Peaceful Revolution of 1989 — anchors a tour through the industrial and artistic heartland of Saxony. Chemnitz, Zwickau, and the Erzgebirge mining towns round out the picture.
highlights: Leipzig St Thomas Church Bach concerts
trips.cover_search_query: Leipzig Nikolaikirche Peaceful Revolution Germany

trips.name: Görlitz & Lusatia
trips.description: Görlitz escaped bombing in both World Wars and preserves one of Central Europe's most complete old town ensembles. The route explores Lusatia's Sorbian minority culture, the Zittau Mountains, and the Neisse River border landscape.
trips.cover_search_query: Görlitz old town Untermarkt Germany

trips.name: Erzgebirge Mining Heritage
trips.description: The Ore Mountains along the Czech border built Saxon wealth from silver mining and later became a UNESCO World Heritage landscape. Christmas pyramid carvers, lace-makers, and nutcracker craftsmen still fill these hilltop towns.
trips.cover_search_query: Erzgebirge Ore Mountains Christmas wooden crafts Germany

trips.name: Saxony Beyond Dresden
trips.description: Once you leave Dresden, Saxony rewards with the spa town of Bad Muskau and its UNESCO Muskau Park, the Sorbian market town of Bautzen, and the porcelain legacy of Meissen. This loop reveals the width and depth of Saxon culture.
trips.cover_search_query: Dresden Frauenkirche skyline Elbe River Germany

trips.name: Thuringia — Classical Heritage
trips.description: Weimar and Erfurt form the twin poles of Thuringia's extraordinary cultural legacy — Goethe and Schiller in Weimar, the reformer Martin Luther in Erfurt. Between them lies a gentle landscape of forest, river valley, and small Thuringian towns.
trips.cover_search_query: Weimar Goethe house Thuringia Germany

trips.name: Wartburg & Eisenach
trips.description: The Wartburg above Eisenach is one of Germany's greatest castles — where Martin Luther translated the New Testament and Richard Wagner set Tannhäuser. Bach was also born in Eisenach, making this a place of extraordinary cultural density.
trips.cover_search_query: Wartburg Castle Eisenach Thuringia Germany autumn

trips.name: Thuringian Forest Ridge Road
trips.description: The Rennsteig is the historic ridge road along the Thuringian Forest crest, once a border between dialects and kingdoms. Now it is Germany's favourite long-distance hiking trail, and this drive follows it through dark spruce forests and timber-frame spa towns.
trips.cover_search_query: Rennsteig Thuringian Forest ridge path Germany

trips.name: Saale-Unstrut Wine Valley
trips.description: Germany's northernmost wine region clings to the steep terraces of the Saale and Unstrut rivers. Limestone cliffs, ruined castles, and the Romanesque cathedral of Naumburg frame a wine landscape of haunting beauty rarely visited by international tourists.
trips.cover_search_query: Saale-Unstrut Freyburg wine terraces river Germany

trips.name: Eastern Thuringia Porcelain Loop
trips.description: Eastern Thuringia is the birthplace of Thuringian porcelain and glass, with a string of small towns each famous for their craft. Jena's university city contrasts with the delicate manufactures of Rudolstadt and Saalfeld's fairy grottoes.
trips.cover_search_query: Saalfeld Fairy Grottoes minerals Thuringia Germany

trips.name: Harz Mountains Classic
trips.description: The Harz is northern Germany's only real upland, a plateau of dark fir forest, moor bogs, and ancient silver-mining towns. The steam-powered Brocken railway still puffs up to the summit, and the medieval imperial city of Goslar glitters with ore wealth.
trips.cover_search_query: Goslar medieval city Harz Germany

trips.name: Harz Narrow-Gauge Railways
trips.description: The Harz is home to Germany's finest narrow-gauge steam railway network, with three lines criss-crossing the mountains. This route combines riding the trains with driving between stations through the charming spa towns and half-timbered villages.
trips.cover_search_query: Brocken steam railway Harz Germany narrow gauge

trips.name: Quedlinburg & the Romanesque Road
trips.description: The Romanische Straße in Saxony-Anhalt visits the greatest concentration of Romanesque architecture north of the Alps — Quedlinburg, Halberstadt, Naumburg, and Merseburg. This is medieval Germany at its most monumental.
highlights: Gernrode St Cyriakus oldest galleried church
trips.cover_search_query: Quedlinburg UNESCO Stiftskirche castle hill Germany

trips.name: Harz Witches Trail
trips.description: The Harz has a reputation for witchcraft since the Middle Ages, and the route follows the legendary Walpurgis Night tradition from Thale's Hexentanzplatz through the misty forests to the Brocken summit where witches allegedly danced.
highlights: Bad Harzburg cable car and spa
trips.cover_search_query: Brocken summit mist Harz Germany fog

trips.name: Harz Southern Loop
trips.description: The gentler southern Harz dissolves into a limestone karst landscape with cave systems, salt springs, and spa towns. Nordhausen, Sangerhausen, and the Kyffhäuser Monument mark the transition from dark forest to open farmland.
trips.cover_search_query: Kyffhäuser monument Barbarossa Harz Germany

trips.name: Hamburg & Lübeck — Hanseatic Twins
trips.description: Two of the great Hanseatic merchant cities anchor the western end of the Baltic coast drive. Hamburg's massive port contrasts with Lübeck's concentration of brick-Gothic churches and the marzipan-scented alleys of the Holsten Quarter.
trips.cover_search_query: Lübeck Holstentor Hanse Baltic Germany

trips.name: Rügen Island Circuit
trips.description: Germany's largest island is a world of chalk cliffs, beech forests, and Wilhelminian spa architecture. The ghostly Prora holiday complex, the Jasmund National Park chalk cliffs, and the fishing harbour of Sellin frame one of Germany's most distinctive landscapes.
highlights: Kap Arkona lighthouse and Viking fort
trips.cover_search_query: Rügen chalk cliffs Königsstuhl Baltic Germany

trips.name: Mecklenburg Coast & Greifswald
trips.description: The Mecklenburg Baltic coast hops between fishing villages, sand dunes, and brick-Gothic harbour towns. Greifswald's perfectly preserved old town and the monastery ruin on Eldena make this a Caspar David Friedrich landscape come to life.
highlights: Greifswald Caspar David Friedrich birthplace area
trips.cover_search_query: Stralsund Hanseatic harbour Baltic Germany

trips.name: Schleswig-Holstein & Flensburg Fjord
trips.description: At Germany's northern tip, the landscape opens into wide skies, Viking ship burial sites, and the Danish-German border culture around Flensburg. The Schlei fjord, the Viking Museum at Haithabu, and the Flensburg rum trading heritage make this a rich detour.
trips.cover_search_query: Schleswig Holm fishermen village fjord Germany

trips.name: Wadden Sea & North Frisian Islands
trips.description: Germany's UNESCO Wadden Sea National Park is the world's largest tidal flat ecosystem. The North Frisian Islands of Sylt, Föhr, and Amrum are connected to the mainland by tidal causeways and ferry, creating a memorable island-hopping coastal drive.
trips.cover_search_query: Sylt island beach dunes North Sea Germany

trips.name: Usedom & the Stettin Lagoon
trips.description: Usedom is Germany's sunniest island, its chalk-white beach promenades lined with ornate Wilhelminian villas. The Peenemünde rocket development site adds a layer of 20th-century history to this tranquil Baltic island.
trips.cover_search_query: Usedom Heringsdorf Wilhelminian beach pier Baltic Germany

trips.name: Hamburg Waterways & River Country
trips.description: Hamburg's inland waterways extend into the Altes Land orchard country, the Elbe marshes, and the Vierlande market gardens. This loop explores the lesser-known Green Hamburg — canals, dykes, red-brick farmhouses, and Europe's oldest orchards.
trips.cover_search_query: Hamburg Speicherstadt warehouse canal Germany night

trips.name: Mecklenburg Lake District
trips.description: The Mecklenburgische Seenplatte is a vast glacial lake district of over 1,000 lakes linked by the Müritz-Elde waterway. Houseboating, stork villages, and the moated castle at Schwerin define this peaceful corner of northeastern Germany.
trips.cover_search_query: Schwerin Castle island lake Germany

trips.name: Schwerin to Rostock Coastal Loop
trips.description: From Schwerin's fairy-tale castle the route heads north through the flat Mecklenburg countryside to the Baltic coast at Rostock and Warnemünde, returning through the brick-Gothic churches of the Hanseatic towns.
trips.cover_search_query: Wismar Marienkirche Hanse town Germany Baltic

trips.name: Müritz National Park Loop
trips.description: Germany's largest inland national park is a sanctuary of reed fringes, osprey nests, and ancient beech forests surrounding the Müritz lake. This slow loop circles the lake through tiny fishing villages and migratory bird viewing points.
highlights: Röbel medieval town above the lake
trips.cover_search_query: Müritz lake sunrise National Park Germany

trips.name: Havel Lakes & Rheinsberg
trips.description: The Havel River expands into a series of lakes between Potsdam and Müritz, passing the Rococo palace of Rheinsberg where Frederick the Great spent his happiest years. Houseboats, summer theatres, and slow river towns fill this gentle loop.
trips.cover_search_query: Rheinsberg Palace lake Brandenburg Germany

trips.name: Nuremberg & Franconian Heritage
trips.description: Nuremberg is medieval Germany's most impressive surviving city, ringed by walls and crowned with the Kaiserburg. This loop pairs it with the brewing city of Bamberg and the opera city of Bayreuth in a tour of Franconia's cultural high points.
trips.cover_search_query: Bamberg Little Venice Regnitz Germany

trips.name: Franconian Switzerland
trips.description: The Fränkische Schweiz is a miniature landscape of limestone rock towers, hop gardens, and half-timbered villages stuffed between dramatic cliff faces. It has more breweries per capita than any other region in the world.
trips.cover_search_query: Franconian Switzerland limestone rocks Tüchersfeld village Germany

trips.name: Franconian Wine Road
trips.description: Franconian wine comes in distinctive Bocksbeutel flasks and pairs with the region's hearty cuisine. The Main River loops through vine-covered hillsides from Würzburg to Volkach and beyond, with Baroque pilgrimage churches at every turn.
trips.cover_search_query: Würzburg Main River vineyard Franconian wine Germany

trips.name: Altmühltal Nature Park
trips.description: The Altmühl River meanders through one of Germany's largest nature parks in wide loops between white limestone cliffs riddled with fossils. Canoeists and cyclists share the valley with cruising vultures and prehistoric village reconstructions.
trips.cover_search_query: Altmühltal river limestone cliffs Bavaria Germany

trips.name: Steigerwald & Haßberge
trips.description: Between the Main and the Regnitz, the Steigerwald forest hides ancient oak forests, Cistercian monasteries, and the wine town of Iphofen. The Haßberge hills to the north add a further layer of forested tranquillity to this quiet Franconian corner.
trips.cover_search_query: Ebrach Cistercian monastery Steigerwald Germany

trips.name: Nuremberg to Regensburg
trips.description: Two of Germany's finest medieval cities lie just 100km apart on the old imperial road through Franconia. The route between them passes the Ludwig-Donau-Main canal, the Altmühl Valley, and some of the finest Romanesque churches in Germany.
trips.cover_search_query: Regensburg Steinerne Brücke Danube Germany

trips.name: Heidelberg & the Odenwald
trips.description: Heidelberg's ruined castle above the Neckar has inspired poets from Goethe to Twain, and the Alte Brücke and Old Town are Germany's most photographed ensemble. The wooded Odenwald behind the city conceals half-timbered towns and Roman roads.
highlights: Heidelberg Castle ruins and old town
trips.cover_search_query: Heidelberg Castle Neckar River Germany

trips.name: Swabian Alb Limestone Plateau
trips.description: The Schwäbische Alb is a dramatic limestone plateau of dry valleys, juniper heaths, and cliff-top castles. Hohenzollern — ancestral seat of the German emperors — rises from a lone hill in the most theatrical castle setting in Germany.
trips.cover_search_query: Hohenzollern Castle hill mist Swabian Alb Germany

trips.name: Lake Constance Circuit
trips.description: The Bodensee is Germany's largest lake, an inland sea shared with Austria and Switzerland. The German shore combines the medieval island city of Konstanz, apple orchards of Überlingen, and the vine island of Reichenau in a relaxed lakeside drive.
highlights: Konstanz Old Town on the lake
trips.cover_search_query: Lake Constance Meersburg castle Germany summer

trips.name: Stuttgart & Swabian Wine Country
trips.description: Stuttgart sits in a bowl of vineyards on the Neckar, and the Württemberg wine route winds along the river through castles, terraced hillside vineyards, and the ancient Roman fort town of Heilbronn. Cars and wine collide happily here.
trips.cover_search_query: Stuttgart Weinsteige vineyard terrace city Germany

trips.name: Upper Swabia Baroque Route
trips.description: Upper Swabia has the highest concentration of Baroque religious architecture in Germany, a legacy of the Counter-Reformation. The Oberschwäbische Barockstraße links seventeen pilgrimage churches and monasteries in a landscape of rolling moraine hills.
highlights: Weingarten Basilica largest Baroque church south Germany
Ulm Münster world's tallest church spire
trips.cover_search_query: Weingarten Basilica Baroque Upper Swabia Germany

trips.name: Neckar Valley Castle Road
trips.description: The Burgenstraße follows the Neckar from Heidelberg to Heilbronn through Germany's densest concentration of castles per kilometre. Each bend of the river reveals another ruin on the heights, and the valley's half-timbered towns have barely changed in centuries.
trips.cover_search_query: Neckar Valley castles Hirschhorn Germany river

trips.name: Heidelberg to Konstanz Scenic
trips.description: A south-to-north traverse of Baden-Württemberg links the university city of Heidelberg with Lake Constance through the Black Forest heights, the Swabian Alb, and the Hegau volcanic hills. Every landscape type of the southwest in one sweeping drive.
trips.cover_search_query: Heidelberg old town aerial Neckar Germany

trips.name: Berlin & Potsdam Royal Circuit
trips.description: Potsdam's magnificent park and palace ensemble — Sanssouci, Cecilienhof, New Garden — forms one of Europe's greatest royal landscapes, an easy day-trip from Berlin. This circuit extends the Hohenzollern trail along the Havel lakes to Brandenburg an der Havel.
highlights: Sanssouci Frederick the Great's rococo palace
trips.cover_search_query: Sanssouci Palace fountain Potsdam Germany

trips.name: Spreewald Biosphere Reserve
trips.description: The Spreewald is an extraordinary network of waterways where the Spree fans into hundreds of channels through an ancient floodplain forest. Sorbian culture, punter ferries, gherkin farms, and horse-drawn post coaches survive in this UNESCO biosphere.
trips.cover_search_query: Spreewald canal punt boat Lübbenau Germany

trips.name: Fläming & Lutherstadt Wittenberg
trips.description: The gentle Fläming hills south of Berlin offer cycling through glacial moraine landscape to the town where Martin Luther nailed his 95 Theses. Wittenberg's Reformation heritage is entirely walkable, and the Fläming's castle loop adds medieval colour.
trips.cover_search_query: Wittenberg Castle Church Reformation Germany Luther

trips.name: Havel Lakes & Rhinow Hills
trips.description: The Havelland west of Berlin is a lake-scattered plain where Hohenzollern hunting lodges, sleepy fishing villages, and the aviation pioneer Otto Lilienthal's home hills create an unexpectedly diverse short break from the capital.
trips.cover_search_query: Havel River lakes Brandenburg Germany spring blossom

trips.name: Lausitz — Sorbian Country
trips.description: The Lusatia region straddles Saxony and Brandenburg and is home to the Sorbs, Germany's only indigenous Slavic minority, with their own language, painted Easter eggs, and folk costumes. The lignite mining transformation here is also extraordinary.
highlights: Cottbus Branitz palace and Pückler landscape
trips.cover_search_query: Bautzen Sorbian town Lusatia Germany

trips.name: Eifel Volcanic Lakes
trips.description: The Eifel is a Volcanic Field of extinct maar craters now filled with deep blue lakes. The Laacher See, Pulvermaar, and Gemündener Maar offer eerie geological beauty, while Nürburg's legendary race circuit draws motorsport pilgrims.
trips.cover_search_query: Nürburgring Nordschleife Eifel race circuit Germany

trips.name: Hunsrück High Road
trips.description: The Hunsrück plateau between Moselle and Rhine is crossed by an ancient ridge road past the Erbeskopf summit, the Celtic ring forts at Otzenhausen, and the Hunnen-Ring. Edgar Reitz set his epic Heimat film cycle in these atmospheric forested hills.
highlights: Idar-Oberstein cliff church in rock face
trips.cover_search_query: Idar-Oberstein church cliff rock face Germany

trips.name: Cochem & Moselle-Eifel
trips.description: Where the Moselle cuts through the Eifel it forms the most dramatic section of its entire course, with Cochem's imperial castle looming over the river and the wine villages of Bremm and Alken perched on near-vertical slate slopes.
trips.cover_search_query: Cochem Imperial Castle Moselle River Germany

trips.name: Eifel National Park & Aachen
trips.description: The Eifel National Park in the north contains the last primeval beech forest remnants of western Germany. Charlemagne's capital at Aachen — its cathedral a UNESCO World Heritage treasure — anchors this westernmost corner of Germany.
trips.cover_search_query: Monschau half-timbered Eifel valley Germany

trips.name: Lüneburg Heath
trips.description: In late summer the Lüneburg Heath turns purple with heather, creating one of Germany's most distinctive landscapes. The salt city of Lüneburg, the Bergen-Belsen memorial, and the horse town of Celle frame the heath in history and natural beauty.
highlights: Lüneburg salt works and leaning buildings
Celle ducal palace and old town
trips.cover_search_query: Lüneburg Heath heather bloom purple August Germany

trips.name: Bremen & the Weser Marshes
trips.description: Bremen's magnificent Marktplatz with its Roland statue and Town Musicians sculpture anchors a route through the Weser Marshes, the half-timbered masterpiece of Worpswede artists' colony, and the Elbe mouth at Cuxhaven.
highlights: Bremen Roland statue and Town Musicians
trips.cover_search_query: Bremen Marktplatz Rathaus Roland Germany

trips.name: East Frisian Islands
trips.description: Seven car-free islands protect the East Frisian coast from the North Sea. Each has its own character — Borkum the grand, Norderney the fashionable, Juist the quietest, Spiekeroog the most traditional — reachable by ferry from the mainland harbours.
trips.cover_search_query: Norderney East Frisian Island lighthouse Germany North Sea

trips.name: Hannover & the Weser Hills
trips.description: The Weserbergland south of Hannover is the setting for the Pied Piper legend of Hameln and the sandstone gorges of the Weser. Half-timbered market towns of exceptional quality — Hameln, Höxter, Corvey Abbey — fill a compact and rewarding loop.
trips.cover_search_query: Hameln Weser Renaissance half-timbered Germany
